description: Capability map for Ship registry v0.1. Targets are implementation surfaces. Features are atomic deliverables with acceptance criteria. All v0.1 capabilities are required for the milestone.

manifest.description: ship.toml — three sections only: [module], [dependencies], [exports]. Located at .ship/ship.toml. TOML only.

acceptance_criteria: [module].name is required — namespaced path string (e.g. github.com/owner/repo)
[module].version is required — semver string, no 'v' prefix in manifest
[module].description is optional string
[module].license is optional SPDX identifier string
Missing name or version → hard parse error with location
Invalid semver → hard parse error
Keys are package paths (strings)
Values: inline table with 'version' (required) and 'grant' (optional string array)
Shorthand: string value = version only, no grants
Version forms accepted: semver range (^, ~, =, >=, <=, >), branch name, 40-char hex SHA
Invalid version constraint → parse error naming the dep
Empty [dependencies] is valid
[exports].skills — optional array of paths relative to .ship/
[exports].agents — optional array of paths relative to .ship/
Omitting [exports] is valid (no public surface)
Each exported skill path must point to a directory containing SKILL.md — validated at ship publish time, not parse time
Each exported agent path must end in .toml — validated at ship publish time

lockfile_format.description: ship.lock — direct deps only for v0.1, TOML, committed alongside ship.toml
acceptance_criteria: Located at .ship/ship.lock
Top-level: version = 1
[[package]] entries: path (string), version (string), commit (40-char hex), hash ('sha256:' prefixed hex)
Only direct dependencies locked in v0.1 (transitive deferred to v0.2)
Human-readable TOML, deterministic field order
Modifying ship.toml deps without running ship install → mismatch error listing added/removed deps

ship_state_rename.description: Rename existing .ship/ship.lock workspace-state file to .ship/ship.state to free the path for the registry lockfile
acceptance_criteria: .ship/ship.state contains: active_profile, compiled_at, plugins (same fields as old ship.lock)
All code that reads/writes ship.lock workspace state is updated to use ship.state
ship use and ship compile read/write ship.state not ship.lock
ship.lock is now exclusively the registry dependency lockfile
No data loss — existing ship.lock content migrated to ship.state on first run

agent_format.description: Agent TOML definition — .ship/agents/<name>.toml. Publishable composition: rules + skills + permissions + MCP + provider config.

acceptance_criteria: [agent].name matches filename without .toml extension
[agent].description is optional
rules = array of strings (local .ship/-relative paths or dep references 'github.com/owner/pkg/rules/file.md')
skills = array of strings (local .ship/-relative paths or dep references 'github.com/owner/pkg/skills/name')
An agent with only rules and no skills is valid
An agent with only skills and no rules is valid
Missing [agent].name → error
[permissions].allow = array of tool permission strings
Empty or absent [permissions] is valid (agent uses no tools)
Permission string formats: 'Bash', 'Bash(git:*)', 'Bash(npm:install)', 'Read', 'Write', 'Glob', 'WebFetch', '*'
Unknown permission string is stored as-is (forward compat) with a warning
[[mcp]].id required string
[[mcp]].command required string
[[mcp]].args optional string array
[[mcp]].env optional string map
Env values support ${VAR} substitution at compile time
Multiple [[mcp]] entries supported
[providers].targets = string array of provider ids
[providers.<name>] per-provider override tables supported
[providers.claude].model optional string
[providers.claude].context_window optional integer
Unknown provider override keys stored as-is

resolver.description: Dependency resolver — direct deps only. Reads ship.toml [dependencies], resolves to exact commits, writes ship.lock. Direct git fetch only (no proxy in v0.1).

version_constraint_parsing.description: Parse and evaluate version constraints against available tags
acceptance_criteria: Semver ranges: ^, ~, =, >=, <=, > evaluate correctly per semver spec
Tags with and without 'v' prefix are treated as equivalent (v1.0.0 == 1.0.0)
Branch name constraint resolves to current HEAD commit of that branch
40-char hex SHA constraint used verbatim
No matching tag → error listing available tags
Resolution is deterministic given the same remote state

git_fetch.description: Fetch package content from git for a specific commit
acceptance_criteria: Fetch via git archive or git clone --depth=1 for the resolved commit
Works with GitHub, GitLab, Codeberg, self-hosted git
Auth via git credential store (no Ship-specific auth in v0.1)
Network error → clear error, no partial state written
Fetch respects git config for proxies, SSH keys

content_hash.description: Compute deterministic SHA-256 content hash for a package tree
acceptance_criteria: Hash algorithm: SHA-256 over sorted file list: for each file (sorted by path): path + NUL + length + NUL + sha256(content), concatenated, then SHA-256 of the whole
Excluded from hash: .git/, .DS_Store, Thumbs.db, *.swp, ship.lock
Two fetches of the same commit always produce identical hash
Hash stored as 'sha256:<lowercase-hex>'
Hash mismatch vs lockfile → hard error, do not use cached content
Writes [[package]] entries for all direct deps in ship.toml [dependencies]
Fields: path, version, commit, hash
Output is deterministic — same inputs = identical file bytes
Existing lockfile replaced atomically (write temp + rename)
ship install with existing valid lockfile skips resolution entirely

lockfile_mismatch_detection.description: Detect when ship.toml and ship.lock are out of sync
acceptance_criteria: On every ship install: compare [dependencies] keys with [[package]] paths in ship.lock
Added dep (in toml, not in lock) → error listing the new dep, suggest 'ship install'
Removed dep (in lock, not in toml) → error listing removed dep, suggest 'ship install'
ship install --frozen fails if lockfile would change (for CI)

cache.description: Content-addressed package cache at ~/.ship/cache/. pnpm model: one entry per unique content hash, shared across all projects.

cache_store.description: Store fetched package content by content hash
acceptance_criteria: Cache root: ~/.ship/cache/objects/<sha256-hex>/
Package files stored verbatim under the hash directory
Two packages with identical content share exactly one cache entry
Write is atomic — partial writes on crash do not leave corrupt entries
Directory created on first use

cache_lookup.description: Look up cached packages before fetching
acceptance_criteria: Cache index: ~/.ship/cache/index/<url-encoded-dep-path>@<version> → sha256 hex
Cache hit: verify stored content hash matches index entry before returning
Cache miss: proceed to git fetch
Hash mismatch (corruption): delete entry, re-fetch, do not use bad content
Warm cache + unchanged lockfile = zero network requests

permissions_enforcement.description: Compiler validates that every required tool permission from every referenced skill is explicitly granted by the consuming agent. Hard error on violation.

acceptance_criteria: Parse YAML frontmatter in SKILL.md
allowed-tools field: space-delimited string → Vec<String>
Absent allowed-tools → empty (skill requires no tools)
Unknown tool strings preserved as-is

compiler_grant_check.description: Validate agent grants cover all skill requirements at compile time
acceptance_criteria: Collect allowed-tools from every skill referenced by the agent
Check each required tool is in agent [permissions].allow
Missing grant → compilation error naming the skill, the tool, and the fix
No transitive escalation: skill A uses skill B's allowed-tools only if skill A also declares them
Passes with zero skills (no tools required)
Passes when permissions.allow contains '*' (grants all)

dep_grant_propagation.description: Permission grants declared in ship.toml [dependencies] are applied to agent compilation
acceptance_criteria: dep { grant = ["Bash(git:*)"] } in ship.toml propagates as if agent listed those in [permissions].allow for that dep's skills only
Grant scope is per-dep — grants for dep A do not apply to dep B's skills
Grants in ship.toml supplement (not replace) grants in agent TOML

cli.description: CLI commands for v0.1: ship install, ship add, ship use. Idempotent. Non-zero exit on error with actionable message.

ship_install.description: ship install — resolve (or use lockfile) + fetch to cache + compile to provider targets
acceptance_criteria: No ship.toml → error 'No ship.toml found. Run ship init or create .ship/ship.toml'
ship.toml + ship.lock in sync → fetch missing packages, compile, exit 0
ship.toml + ship.lock out of sync → error listing delta, suggest 'ship install' (which re-resolves)
No ship.lock → resolve from constraints, write ship.lock, fetch, compile
ship install --frozen → fail if lockfile would change
All provider target files written to correct locations
Idempotent: run twice with no changes → identical output

ship_add.description: ship add <path>[@version] — add dependency and install
acceptance_criteria: ship add github.com/owner/repo → adds to [dependencies] with version='main', resolves, updates lock, compiles
ship add github.com/owner/repo@^1.0.0 → uses provided constraint
ship add github.com/owner/repo@abc123 → uses commit SHA
Duplicate dep → error 'already in dependencies'
Resolution failure → error, ship.toml unchanged
On success: ship.toml updated, ship.lock updated, provider targets recompiled

ship_use.description: ship use [<preset>] — activate a preset/agent and compile (existing command, update for new terminology)
acceptance_criteria: Existing ship use behavior preserved
Writes ship.state (not ship.lock) for workspace state
ship use with no args lists available agents
ship use <name> activates agent, compiles provider targets
